subject: Re: Some issues with tilt series processing
body:
hi Benjamin

thank you. I updated to nightly build and it worked!    FYI it doesn't look 
like the excluding views was the issue because I did not have to do that 
for my data.    Thanks!



On Monday, October 25, 2021 at 12:00:01 PM UTC-7 benjami...@gmail.com wrote:

> For extra context, our error happened because we were "excluding views" 
> and removing those excluded views at the first step of etomo, since we had 
> some highly occluded high angle views (frost and lamella geometry are 
> tough!).
>
> Benjamin Barad, PhD
> Postdoctoral Fellow
> Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
> He/His/Him
>
>
> On Mon, Oct 25, 2021 at 11:58 AM Benjamin Barad <benjami...@gmail.com> 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Jesse (and others encountering this issue in the future),
>>
>> Dimitry fixed the bug - we've since been able to work with cut views in 
>> multiple nightly releases since the August 2nd 2020 release (it actually is 
>> a preferable workflow for me to excluding views later in etomo because warp 
>> doesn't waste time processing tilts I'll never use) - what version are you 
>> using? We've been using the most recent 11/4/2020 nightly for a while (
>> http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/) and haven't run into any serious 
>> bugs, so I'd say that suggests it is pretty stable!
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Ben
>>
>> Benjamin Barad, PhD
>> Postdoctoral Fellow
>> Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
>> He/His/Him
>>
>>
>> On Mon, Oct 25, 2021 at 11:47 AM mrJ <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> hey Dimitry,
>>>
>>> did this ever get sorted out?  No idea why this is happening to me now.  
>>> I have 7 tilt series processing in parallel.  Went through the pipeline as 
>>> described by Benjamin, however only 2 of the 7 reimport into WARP.  The 
>>> other 5 I get the error he described.  Any help would be really 
>>> appreciated.  I tried reprocessing the data entirely and arrived at the 
>>> same outcome :(
>>>
>>> Jesse
>>>
>>> On Saturday, July 25, 2020 at 4:06:54 AM UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>
>>>> Weird! Can you come up with a minimal set of Warp's XML metadata (no 
>>>> corresponding image data needed), MDOCs, IMOD alignments and the necessary 
>>>> folder structure to reproduce it, and send it to me?
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Saturday, July 25, 2020 at 1:05:53 AM UTC+2, Benjamin Barad wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>>>
>>>>> I am trying to process some older tilt series data through warp, and 
>>>>> running into an interesting error. The data was collected at a period of 
>>>>> time when we were saving frames into individual folders for each tilt 
>>>>> series, so in order to better match the workflow of warp I moved a couple 
>>>>> of tilt series frames to a single folder (out of their subfolders from 
>>>>> serialEM). I did the same for the mdoc files for the whole tilt series. I 
>>>>> am able to do frame processing, export to imod and perform alignment in 
>>>>> etomo, but when trying to bring the alignment files back into warp to make 
>>>>> tomostars, I get a pair of the following errors that prevent import: 
>>>>> "Object reference not set to an instance of an object."
>>>>>
>>>>> Is this because I moved the files around in a non-ideal way? Do I need 
>>>>> to preserve the original relative folder structure? Since warp succeeded in 
>>>>> everything up to and including exporting to etomo, I am a little surprised 
>>>>> to run into these errors this far down the line. Another possible issue is 
>>>>> that we have recalibrated the pixel size since these were collected, and I 
>>>>> manually updated the pixel size during frame alignment - but again, I would 
>>>>> have expected the issue to arise when the rawtlt and st files were made out 
>>>>> of the frames and mdoc files.
>>>>>
>>>>> I should add that I've gotten newer data (collected to match the 
>>>>> folder structure of EMD-10164) through the pipeline successfully, which is 
>>>>> why I suspect the issue is one of these two differences with the old data.
>>>>>
>>>>> Benjamin Barad, PhD
>>>>> Postdoctoral Associate
>>>>> Grotjahn Lab @ Scripps Research
>>>>> He/His/Him
>>>>>
>>>> -- 
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>>> To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/55ebcedc-2cde-41ba-b8d9-76246649ace1n%40googlegroups.com 
>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/55ebcedc-2cde-41ba-b8d9-76246649ace1n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>>
